one_line_takeaway: A theoretically grounded LLM watermarking framework that jointly optimizes scheme and detector, showing the optimal watermark signal should adapt to the model's own token distribution, and realizes it as DAWA -- a distortion-free, surrogate-model detector that dominates baselines at ultra-low FPR and on low-entropy text.
key_finding: DAWA's advantage is largest at ultra-low FPR and on low-entropy text, matching the theory that low-entropy tokens should be mapped to the redundant auxiliary symbol to avoid false positives. In Figure 1 (Llama2-13B, Wikipedia), DAWA reaches TPR=0.882 at FPR=1e-5, versus 0.690 (KGW+23), 0.515 (Gumbel-Max), 0.862 (EXP-edit), and 0.259 (HCW+23). It also stays strongest under a 50%-token-replacement attack (e.g. Llama2-13B/C4 ROC-AUC 0.989 vs HCW+23's 0.890) while keeping text quality near the unwatermarked baseline (BLEU 0.214 vs human 0.219; perplexity 10.034 vs unwatermarked 10.020 and human 8.846, whereas KGW+23 degrades to 13.472).
core_contribution: The paper's OWN contribution is a unified theoretical framework for LLM watermarking that JOINTLY optimizes the watermarking scheme and the detector (Sections 2-3), whereas the authors explicitly note prior theoretical work optimized only one side: Takezawa et al./Wouters/Cai et al. optimize the logit-perturbation of the green-red scheme, Huang et al. [42] derive an optimal scheme for a FIXED detector, and Li et al. [43] optimize the detector for a FIXED scheme. The authors cast watermarking as an independence test between generated text X_1^T and a shared auxiliary random sequence zeta_1^T, then solve the constrained optimization (Opt-O): minimize Type-II error (1-TPR) subject to a worst-case FPR cap alpha and a distortion cap epsilon. Their Theorem 1 gives the closed-form 'universally minimum Type-II error' (over ALL detectors and schemes, not just a fixed one), and Theorem 2 gives the closed-form jointly optimal scheme-detector pair. The central theoretical insight claimed as novel: the optimal watermark signal should be ADAPTIVE to the LLM's own generative (next-token-prediction) distribution, unlike existing schemes that sample the auxiliary variable from a fixed uniform distribution. Sections 4-5 translate this sequence-level optimum into a practical token-level scheme and the DAWA (Distribution-Adaptive Watermarking Algorithm), which is distortion-free (epsilon=0) and uses a surrogate language model plus the Gumbel-Max trick to recover the auxiliary sequence at detection without access to the original LLM or prompt (model-agnostic).
threat_model: Watermarking is framed as a defense for attributing text provenance: distinguishing AI-generated text from human-written text to counter plagiarism, content-accountability, and misuse (stated in the Introduction; publishers, educators, regulators are named beneficiaries). Two adversarial pressures are modeled by the paper itself: (1) the risk of FALSELY accusing a human author, which motivates controlling the worst-case false positive rate at ultra-low levels (e.g. 1e-05); the detector is required to be model-agnostic (no access to original LLM or prompt). (2) Watermark-removal / evasion attacks by a user trying to strip the watermark: the paper proves and empirically tests robustness against token-replacement attacks (Proposition 4), and additionally evaluates random-deletion and paraphrasing attacks (Appendix J.1). Appendix J sketches a theoretical extension to semantic-invariant removal attacks. It is NOT a threat model about compromising the LLM itself; the security frame is content-provenance/anti-misuse, which is genuine to the paper (cs.CR).
limitations: (1) Theorem 2's exact optimal construction is intractable to implement directly: it requires a bijection g over a token-sequence space of size |V|^T that grows exponentially, is derived for a FIXED length T (not incremental generation), and assumes full recovery of the auxiliary sequence at detection -- the paper itself enumerates these as challenges 1-3 and only APPROXIMATES the optimum with the token-level DAWA relaxation, which it concedes 'may not be optimal at the sequence level.' (2) Detection depends on a surrogate LM that shares the tokenizer and approximates the watermarked NTP distribution; robustness to surrogate mismatch is only shown in ablations (Appendix I), and approximation error is argued benign via the token-replacement robustness result rather than bounded directly. (3) Empirical scope is two model families, three-plus datasets, T=200, single eta=0.2; the ultra-low-FPR curve uses only 105 texts, which limits the resolution of a 1e-5 FPR estimate. (4) Strong robustness to semantic/paraphrase attacks is largely theoretical (Appendix J); on paraphrasing DAWA only 'matches' Gumbel-Max and KGW+23 rather than beating them.

datasets_used: Wikipedia (105 texts of length 200, used for the ultra-low-FPR TPR curve in Figure 1)
WMT19 machine-translation dataset (with mBART, for BLEU evaluation)
quantitative_results: Figure 1 (Llama2-13B, Wikipedia) TPR@1e-5 FPR: DAWA 0.882, KGW+23 0.690, Gumbel-Max 0.515, EXP-edit 0.862, HCW+23 0.259
Figure 1 TPR@1e-3 FPR: DAWA 0.992, KGW+23 0.942, Gumbel-Max 0.955, EXP-edit 0.882, HCW+23 0.658
Table 1 Llama2-13B / C4 clean text: DAWA ROC-AUC 0.999, TPR@1%FPR 0.998, TPR@10%FPR 1.000
Table 1 Llama2-13B / ELI5 clean text: DAWA ROC-AUC 0.998, TPR@1%FPR 0.997, TPR@10%FPR 1.000
Table 1 Llama2-13B / C4 under 50% token-replacement attack: DAWA ROC-AUC 0.989 vs HCW+23 0.890, KGW+23 0.965
Table 1 Mistral-8x7B / ELI5 under token-replacement: DAWA ROC-AUC 0.993, TPR@1%FPR 0.991
Table 2 empirical entropy: C4 vs ELI5 = 0.547 vs 0.272 (Llama2-13B), 1.475 vs 1.427 (Mistral-8x7B)
Table 3 BLEU score (higher better): Human 0.219, DAWA 0.214, Gumbel-Max 0.210, HCW+23 0.207, EXP-Edit 0.203, KGW+23 0.158
Table 3 Perplexity (lower better): Human 8.846, unwatermarked 10.020, DAWA 10.034, HCW+23 10.115, EXP-Edit 10.126, Gumbel-Max 9.910, KGW+23 13.472
DAWA hyperparameters: token-level FPR eta=0.2, generation length T=200
baselines_compared: KGW+23 (Kirchenbauer et al. green-red list watermark, watermark strength delta=2, prior-1-token hash)
EXP-edit (Kuditipudi et al., inverse-transform distortion-free watermark)
